header.title: PDF Merger — Combine PDF Files into One Online Free
header.description: Free online PDF merger to combine multiple PDF files into a single document. Drag, reorder, and select specific pages from each file before merging. Join PDFs quickly with custom page selection — 100% client-side, your files stay private.
header.keywords: pdf merger, combine pdf, merge pdf files, join pdf, pdf combiner, merge pdfs online, combine pdf documents, free pdf merger

privacy.notice: 100% client-side. Your PDFs are processed locally using pdf-lib — no files are uploaded to any server.

instructions.title: How to use
instructions.step1: Drop or upload 2 or more PDF files
instructions.step3: Optionally select custom page ranges per file

howToUse.title: How to Use PDF Merger
howToUse.step1Desc: Drop or upload two or more PDF files. You can add multiple files at once.
howToUse.step2Desc: Drag to reorder files. Optionally select specific page ranges from each file instead of merging all pages.
howToUse.step3Desc: Check the file count, total pages, and total size before merging.
howToUse.step4Desc: Click 'Merge & Download PDF' to combine all files into a single document — 100% client-side.

items.question: How many PDF files can I merge at once?
items.answer: There is no set limit, but browser performance may decrease with very large numbers of files. Most users merge 2-10 files comfortably.

items.question: Can I select specific pages from each file?
items.answer: Yes. Click on a file to expand page range options. You can enter custom ranges like '1-3, 5' to include only certain pages.

items.question: What is the maximum file size for merging?
items.answer: Files up to 100 MB typically work well. Extremely large files may hit browser memory limits since everything runs client-side.

items.question: Does merging preserve bookmarks and links?
items.answer: Basic merging preserves page content. Some PDF features like bookmarks, internal links, and form fields may not carry over perfectly.

items.question: Can I merge password-protected PDFs?
items.answer: No. You must remove passwords from protected PDFs first using the Unlock PDF tool before merging.

items.question: Is my data uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. All merging is done locally in your browser using pdf-lib. Your files never leave your device.

items.question: What happens to duplicate page sizes?
items.answer: Each page retains its original dimensions. The merged PDF will contain pages of varying sizes if the source files use different page sizes.
